reasoning: Magnus Carlsen has held the FIDE #1 ranking continuously since 2011, maintaining a consistent Elo rating gap over his closest competitors (e.g., Fabiano Caruana, Hikaru Nakamura). Since FIDE officially standardizes and publishes rankings on a monthly basis, the ranking on the first resolution date (2025-11-16) will almost certainly be identical to the ranking on 2025-11-09, as both fall within the same month's rating list period. Wikipedia updates its main FIDE rankings page based on these monthly official releases. For the subsequent resolution dates (1 month and 3 months out), the likelihood of another player overcoming a ~20-30 point Elo deficit within such a brief timeframe is historically exceptionally low. Barring extreme and unprecedented events, Carlsen will retain the world #1 rank. I am predicting 0.95 for the same-month date, gradually lowering to 0.89 for the 3-month horizon to appropriately reflect slightly increasing uncertainty over time.
